description: Someone 'nishoku' when irritation in the nose triggers a sudden involuntary burst of air through the nose and mouth. 'kehota' (cough) begins deeper in the airway and chiefly leaves through the mouth. The cause of a sneeze belongs to the surrounding account.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for dental 'n', carry the voice through the nose, and release into high-front unrounded 'i'. Raise the tongue blade towards the postalveolar region for unvoiced 'sh' and round the lips around stressed middle-height back 'o'. Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for unvoiced 'k' and finish with high-back rounded 'u'.
usage_notes: 'nishoku' takes no object in ordinary use. In noun position, the form is one sneeze; aspect or a count shows repetition. Its cause belongs in another clause.

semantic_domains.physical: an involuntary burst of air prompted by irritation in the nose
